causal_chain.from: Increased kinetic energy of water molecules due to heating
causal_chain.mechanism: Heat increases molecular motion, weakening the bonds holding the water molecules together in a liquid state.

causal_chain.to: Vapor pressure of water equals atmospheric pressure
causal_chain.mechanism: As more molecules escape into the gaseous phase, the vapor pressure increases until it matches the surrounding atmospheric pressure.

causal_chain.from: Atmospheric pressure is at sea level standard
causal_chain.mechanism: By definition, the boiling point is the temperature at which vapor pressure equals atmospheric pressure. At standard sea level pressure, this occurs at 100 degrees Celsius for pure water.
